message:
fix: Resolve all 25 test failures (metadata finder + pocketfft -fPIC)

1. _wasi_compat.py: Rewrite ZipMetadataFinder to use zipimport.zipimporter
   instead of zipfile.ZipFile. The zipimporter already loaded the zip for
   module imports — piggyback on its _files dict and get_data() method.
   zipfile.ZipFile() fails silently in WASI VFS because re-opening the
   zip file as a regular file doesn't work through the VFS layer.
   Fixes: pydantic, langchain, langgraph, ai-libs, fastapi tests (16)

2. build-numpy-wasm.sh: Add -fPIC to FFT_CFLAGS for pocketfft compilation.
   Was the only CFLAGS missing -fPIC, causing wasm-ld relocation errors
   that prevented the numpy variant from linking.
   Fixes: real-world numpy tests (9)

3. test.yml: Remove continue-on-error and simplify variant build step.
   Builds should pass without fallbacks now.

files.patch:
@@ -91,7 +91,6 @@ jobs:
           key: numpy-wasm-${{ hashFiles('scripts/build-numpy-wasm.sh', 'recipes/numpy.json') }}
 
       - name: Build numpy WASM objects
-        continue-on-error: true
         run: |
           if [ ! -d build/zig-wasi/Modules/numpy ] || [ "$(ls build/zig-wasi/Modules/numpy/*.o 2>/dev/null | wc -l)" -eq 0 ]; then
             bash scripts/build-numpy-wasm.sh
@@ -104,25 +103,15 @@ jobs:
           key: pydantic-core-wasm-${{ hashFiles('scripts/build-pydantic-core-wasm.sh', 'recipes/pydantic-core.json') }}
 
       - name: Build pydantic-core WASM objects
-        continue-on-error: true
         run: |
           if [ ! -d build/recipes/pydantic-core/obj ] || [ "$(ls build/recipes/pydantic-core/obj/*.o build/recipes/pydantic-core/obj/*.a 2>/dev/null | wc -l)" -eq 0 ]; then
             bash scripts/build-pydantic-core-wasm.sh
           fi
 
-      - name: Build variant python.wasm (pydantic-core, then try +numpy)
+      - name: Build variant python.wasm (numpy + pydantic-core)
         run: |
-          # Build pydantic-core only variant first (guaranteed to work)
-          bash scripts/build-variant.sh pydantic-core
-          cp worker/src/python-pydantic-core.wasm worker/src/python.wasm
-
-          # Attempt numpy+pydantic-core variant (may fail due to incomplete numpy objects)
-          if bash scripts/build-variant.sh numpy pydantic-core; then
-            echo "Full variant built successfully — using numpy+pydantic-core"
-            cp worker/src/python-numpy-pydantic-core.wasm worker/src/python.wasm
-          else
-            echo "WARNING: numpy variant link failed — using pydantic-core only"
-          fi
+          bash scripts/build-variant.sh numpy pydantic-core
+          cp worker/src/python-numpy-pydantic-core.wasm worker/src/python.wasm
 
       - name: Install npm dependencies
         run: npm install

@@ -60,63 +60,80 @@ class _Uname:
 
 # Make importlib.metadata work with packages inside zip files.
 # Packages in site-packages.zip include dist-info/METADATA but the default
-# MetadataPathFinder can't look inside zips. Register a custom finder.
+# MetadataPathFinder can't look inside zips via the WASI VFS (zipfile.ZipFile
+# re-open fails). Use zipimport.zipimporter which already loaded these zips.
 def _install_zip_metadata_finder():
     import importlib.metadata as metadata
-    import sys
-    import zipfile
+    import zipimport
     import pathlib
+    import os
 
     def _normalize(name):
         return name.lower().replace("-", "_").replace(".", "_")
 
     class ZipDistribution(metadata.Distribution):
-        def __init__(self, zf, dist_dir):
-            self._zf = zf
+        def __init__(self, importer, archive, dist_dir):
+            self._importer = importer
+            self._archive = archive
             self._dist_dir = dist_dir
 
         def read_text(self, filename):
-            path = f"{self._dist_dir}/{filename}"
+            full_path = os.path.join(self._archive, self._dist_dir, filename)
             try:
-                return self._zf.read(path).decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
-            except KeyError:
+                return self._importer.get_data(full_path).decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
+            except (OSError, IOError):
                 return None
 
         def locate_file(self, path):
             return pathlib.PurePosixPath(path)
 
     class ZipMetadataFinder(metadata.DistributionFinder):
-        """Finds package metadata inside zip files on sys.path."""
+        """Finds package metadata inside zip files on sys.path.
+
+        Uses zipimport.zipimporter (already loaded by Python for module imports)
+        instead of zipfile.ZipFile (which requires re-opening the file via VFS).
+        """
 
         @classmethod
         def find_distributions(cls, context=metadata.DistributionFinder.Context()):
             name = getattr(context, "name", None)
             for path_entry in sys.path:
                 if not path_entry.endswith(".zip"):
                     continue
+                # Get the zipimporter from cache (Python already created one for imports)
+                importer = sys.path_importer_cache.get(path_entry)
+                if importer is None:
+                    try:
+                        importer = zipimport.zipimporter(path_entry)
+                    except (OSError, zipimport.ZipImportError):
+                        continue
+                if not isinstance(importer, zipimport.zipimporter):
+                    continue
+                # Scan the zip's file list for .dist-info directories
                 try:
-                    zf = zipfile.ZipFile(path_entry)
-                except (OSError, zipfile.BadZipFile):
+                    zip_files = importer._files
+                except AttributeError:
                     continue
                 dist_dirs = set()
-                for entry in zf.namelist():
-                    if ".dist-info/" in entry:
-                        dist_dir = entry.split(".dist-info/")[0] + ".dist-info"
+                for fpath in zip_files:
+                    if ".dist-info/" in fpath:
+                        dist_dir = fpath.split(".dist-info/")[0] + ".dist-info"
                         dist_dirs.add(dist_dir)
-                for dist_dir in dist_dirs:
-                    meta_path = f"{dist_dir}/METADATA"
-                    if meta_path not in zf.namelist():
-                        continue
+                for dist_dir in sorted(dist_dirs):
+                    meta_path = os.path.join(path_entry, dist_dir, "METADATA")
                     if name:
-                        meta = zf.read(meta_path).decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
+                        try:
+                            meta = importer.get_data(meta_path).decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
+                        except (OSError, IOError):
+                            continue
                         pkg_name = None
                         for line in meta.splitlines():
                             if line.startswith("Name:"):
                                 pkg_name = line.split(":", 1)[1].strip()
                                 break
                         if pkg_name and _normalize(name) != _normalize(pkg_name):
                             continue
-                    yield ZipDistribution(zf, dist_dir)
+                    yield ZipDistribution(importer, path_entry, dist_dir)
 
     sys.meta_path.append(ZipMetadataFinder)

@@ -383,7 +383,7 @@ fi
 echo "  Compiling numpy.fft..."
 FFT_SRC="$NUMPY_SRC/numpy/fft/_pocketfft_umath.cpp"
 if [ -f "$FFT_SRC" ]; then
-  FFT_CFLAGS="-target wasm32-wasi -c -Os -DNDEBUG -std=c++17 \
+  FFT_CFLAGS="-target wasm32-wasi -c -fPIC -Os -DNDEBUG -std=c++17 \
     -Drestrict=__restrict__ \
     -I$CPYTHON/Include -I$CPYTHON/Include/cpython -I$PYCONFIG_DIR \
     -I$NUMPY_SRC/numpy/_core/include -I$NUMPY_SRC/numpy/_core/src/common \
